chapter: Chapter 8: Nature of Matter – Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

questions.question: Explain why matter is considered the fundamental substance of the universe.
questions.answer: Matter forms all physical objects because it has mass and occupies space.

questions.question: Why are mixtures easier to separate than compounds?
questions.answer: Mixtures can be separated by physical methods because their components are not chemically bonded.

questions.question: Explain how homogeneous mixtures differ from heterogeneous mixtures.
questions.answer: Homogeneous mixtures have uniform composition while heterogeneous mixtures have uneven distribution of components.

questions.question: Why is air considered a homogeneous mixture?
questions.answer: Because its gases are uniformly distributed and cannot be distinguished easily.

questions.question: What chemical reaction occurs in the limewater test for carbon dioxide?
questions.answer: Carbon dioxide reacts with limewater to form calcium carbonate, making the solution milky.

questions.question: Why are pure substances defined differently in science compared to daily life?
questions.answer: Scientifically, pure substances contain only one type of particle, whereas in daily life purity refers to absence of impurities.

questions.question: Explain why elements cannot be broken down chemically.
questions.answer: Elements consist of only one type of atom and cannot be decomposed into simpler substances by chemical reactions.

questions.question: Why do atoms combine to form molecules?
questions.answer: Atoms combine to achieve stable electronic configurations.

questions.question: Explain how molecules differ from atoms.
questions.answer: Atoms are single particles of elements while molecules consist of two or more atoms chemically bonded.

questions.question: Describe the process of electrolysis of water.
questions.answer: Electric current passes through water and decomposes it into hydrogen and oxygen gases.

questions.question: Why is hydrogen collected in larger quantity than oxygen during electrolysis?
questions.answer: Because water contains twice as many hydrogen atoms as oxygen atoms.

questions.question: Explain how compounds are formed from elements.
questions.answer: Elements combine chemically in fixed ratios to form compounds with new properties.

questions.question: Why do compounds have properties different from their constituent elements?
questions.answer: Because chemical reactions rearrange atoms to create new substances.

questions.question: What chemical reaction occurs when iron and sulfur are heated?
questions.answer: Iron reacts with sulfur to form iron sulfide.

questions.question: Why cannot iron be separated from iron sulfide using a magnet?
questions.answer: Because iron sulfide is a compound with different properties from iron.

questions.question: Explain the importance of alloys in daily life.
questions.answer: Alloys are stronger and more durable than pure metals and are widely used in tools and machines.

questions.question: Why are alloys considered mixtures rather than compounds?
questions.answer: Because the metals are physically mixed and not chemically bonded in fixed proportions.

questions.question: Explain how minerals differ from compounds.
questions.answer: Minerals are naturally occurring substances in the Earth while compounds are chemically formed substances.

questions.question: Why are minerals important for industries?
questions.answer: Minerals provide raw materials for producing metals, cement, and other industrial products.

questions.question: Explain how filtration separates mixtures.
questions.answer: Filtration separates insoluble solids from liquids using a filter medium.

questions.question: Why is evaporation used to obtain salt from seawater?
questions.answer: Water evaporates leaving behind solid salt.

questions.question: Explain the principle of crystallization.
questions.answer: Crystallization forms pure solid crystals from a solution when the solvent evaporates or cools.

questions.question: Why are chemical reactions essential in forming compounds?
questions.answer: They rearrange atoms to produce substances with new chemical properties.

questions.question: Explain how matter is classified in this chapter.
questions.answer: Matter is classified into elements, compounds, and mixtures based on composition.

questions.question: Why is the study of matter important in scientific research?
questions.answer: It helps scientists understand materials and develop new technologies and products.
